titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/235

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=198 status=live modified=2017-03-09 21:24:41.320571+00 | translation moa=199 status=live modified=2017-03-09 21:24:44.352366+00

div.p:
M. Rex Scott’. Vhtr’ filio fergus . ⁊ Gileberto fratri eius . ⁊ Rad’ filio Dunegal . ⁊ Duunenaldo
fratri eius . vniuersisque aliis probis suis hominibus totius Galweie ⁊ Cludesdalie . salutem. Sciatis
me dedisse iuste meam firmam pacem Omnibus illis hominibus qui eunt aut ibunt in Galweiam
causa inhospitandi ⁊ inhabitandi Terram de Dunroden  quam fergus Ecclesie Sancte Crucis de
Edeneb’. in perpetuam Elemosianm dedit ⁊ carta sua confirmauit . quam etiam Vhtr’ filius eius
sua carta confirmauit . Quorum etiam cartas  ego ipse carta mea confirmaui. Prohibeo itaque
firmiter . ne quis hominum presumat iniuste uexare aut disturbare quemquam uersus terram
prefatam causa in ea manendi tendentem  aut in terra eadem manentem consensu ⁊ uoluntate
canonicorum predicte Ecclesie  aut de terra illa in fidelitate eorundem recedentem . Nullusque
per alicuius aduocationem siue dominium in eadem terra contra uoluntatem predictorum canonicorum maneat  super meam forisfacturam .x. librarum. Testibus Aluredo abbate de Striuel’ . Rob’ Priore
de Mai . Eng’ Cancell’ . W’ . filio Alani dapifero . Iohanne de Vallibus . Apud Clacmanant .

Malcolm king of Scots to Uhtred son of Fergus, and Gilbert his brother, and Ralph son of Dunegal, and Domnall his brother, and to all his other worthy men of the whole of Galloway and Clydesdale, Greeting: Know that I have justly given my firm peace to all those men who go or shall go to Galloway to lodge or dwell in the Land of Dunrod, which Fergus gave to the church of Holyrood in Edinburgh in perpetual alms and affirmed by his charter, which Uhtred his son also confirmed by his charter, whose charters I myself have also confirmed. I therefore firmly forbid that any man presume unjustly to vex or disturb anyone travelling to the said land by reason of dwelling in it, or dwelling in the same land by consent and will of the canons of the aforesaid church, or departing from that land in the fealty of the same [canons]. And no-one may remain in the same land by anyone’s advowson or lordship against the will of the said canons, on pain of my forfeiture of ten pounds. As witnesses, Alured abbot of Stirling, Robert prior of May, Ingram the chancellor, Walter the steward, son of Alan, John de Vaux. At Clackmannan.
